books.title: Mala onda

books.summary: author: Alberto Fuguet name: Felipe Alfonso average rating: 3.33 book published: 1992 rating: 5 read at: date added: 2025/12/07 shelves: favorites, novels review: Este libro se convirtió en mi cimiento. Me enseñó cómo escribir desde un Chile que aún carga sus heridas, pero que no deja de luchar por superarlas. En sus páginas encontré una forma de transformar el dolor en palabra, y la palabra en fuerza.

books.title: Old Path White Clouds: Walking in the Footsteps of the Buddha
books.author: Thich Nhat Hanh
books.summary: author: Thich Nhat Hanh name: Felipe Alfonso average rating: 4.37 book published: 1991 rating: 5 read at: 2018/12/30 date added: 2025/12/07 shelves: buddhism review: An amazing book — quite a long one, but absolutely worth it. It offers a powerful and truthful portrayal of Buddha’s life, brought to life through a beautifully crafted narrative. It feels immersive, sincere, and profoundly insightful.

books.title: Freedom from the Known
books.author: J. Krishnamurti
books.summary: author: J. Krishnamurti name: Felipe Alfonso average rating: 4.30 book published: 1969 rating: 5 read at: 2018/01/01 date added: 2025/12/07 shelves: review: It’s a very good read — concise yet impactful. It distills Krishnamurti’s core philosophical ideas in a way that feels accessible without losing depth. A great starting point for anyone curious about his thinking.

books.title: In My Father's Den
books.author: Maurice Gee
books.summary: author: Maurice Gee name: Felipe Alfonso average rating: 3.87 book published: 1972 rating: 5 read at: 2019/01/01 date added: 2025/12/07 shelves: favorites, fiction review: It’s absolutely tremendous — a delicate, vividly descriptive narrative that wraps you in its atmosphere. I could feel the air, the places, every subtle moment unfolding around me. I loved every second of it. I’m especially glad I read it while I was in New Zealand — it made the experience even more magical.
